title: machaerium lanceolatum
intro: Machaerium lanceolatum, also known as lanceleaf peacock flower, is a plant species in the pea family (Fabaceae). It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities. The plant's extracts have been found to have health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. Machaerium lanceolatum can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat a range of ailments.
